description:
Advances: mutagen.mutators

Discovered during S7 (mutagen-wrd.9) lane fixture authoring.

## Problem

`test/fixtures/lane_project/lib/lane_fixture/infinite_looper.ex` deliberately uses `:arith`-driven recursion (`count_down(n - 1)` → `count_down(n + 1)`) as the deterministic infinite-loop trigger, while the original mutagen-wrd.9 ticket's S7 risk note prescribed `:case_drop` on a recursive function's base case.

The reason for the deviation, documented at `infinite_looper.ex:18-27`:

> :case_drop on this module's `case` removes the BASE case (the last clause), which leaves the recursive clause whose guard rejects n=0 — that raises a CaseClauseError and the mutation classifies as :killed, not :timeout. The :arith trigger gives the same deterministic infinite recursion without the false signal.

This is a spec-level clarification: the mutators catalog promises that `:case_drop` against the recursive function's base case produces `:timeout`, but the actual runtime behavior is `:killed` via CaseClauseError.

## Resolution

Either:

1. **Spec clarification**: Update `mutagen.mutators` / `mutagen.mutation_pipeline` to acknowledge that `:case_drop` on a guarded `case` head whose recursive clause's guard rejects the base value classifies as `:killed`, and document the correct trigger for deterministic timeout (e.g., `:arith` on the recursive descent).
2. **Mutator refinement**: Refine `:case_drop`'s validate predicate to skip cases where dropping the base clause would produce an inevitable CaseClauseError, OR to also strip the recursive clause's guard so the recursion truly diverges.

Option 1 is cheaper; option 2 is a behavioral change to the mutator catalog.

## Related

- mutagen-wrd.9 (S7 lane fixture, where this deviation was made)
- mutagen-wrd.13 (MutationLoop brutal_kill — sister timeout-path bug)

comments.text:
ORCHESTRATOR: impl-only worker BLOCKED before Phase 0.

The spawned impl worker (general-purpose subagent on Opus) reported that Claude
Code's auto-mode classifier denies its `bw` (beadwork CLI) invocations,
classifying them as Bitwarden credential exploration. The subagent could not
even post `WORKER_ERROR` for the same reason. No code was written, no worktree
created, no commits made.

Note: /Users/mz/dev/go/bin/bw is the beadwork issue tracker (the tool every
ticket-driven skill depends on), NOT the Bitwarden CLI. Naming collision.

Resolution required from user: add an allow rule for `bw` in settings (e.g.
`Bash(bw:*)` or `Bash(/Users/mz/dev/go/bin/bw:*)` under .claude/settings.local.json
or ~/.claude/settings.json). After that, re-invoke /orchestrate-epic wrd and the
loop will re-spawn this worker.

mutagen-wrd.14 left open. Other in-flight tickets (.12 impl, .13 rebase) are not
affected — the orchestrator (this session) has bw access; the subagent
restriction is what's hitting .14's worker.

IMPLEMENTED
Session: eb14f76b-662e-400e-ba6b-22e2f3772aea
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex/eb14f76b-662e-400e-ba6b-22e2f3772aea.jsonl

Commit: d85aa34
Branch: mutagen-wrd.14/case-drop-spec-clarification
Worktree: /Users/mz/dev/mz/mutagen_ex-case-drop-spec-clarification

Files touched:
- .spec/specs/mutators.spec.md (catalog note on entry 6, new r8, new s7, new v5 stub)
- .spec/specs/mutation_pipeline.spec.md (r5 amended, s3 amended to point at :arith)
- lib/mutagen_ex/mutators/case_drop.ex (@moduledoc Runtime classification note)
- test/fixtures/lane_project/lib/lane_fixture/infinite_looper.ex (comment now marks
  :arith as documented contract, not deviation)
- test/mutagen_ex/mutators/case_drop_classification_test.exs (NEW)

Tests added:
- MutagenEx.Mutators.CaseDropClassificationTest (4 tests):
  - guarded-recursive-base-case pattern (mutagen.mutators.r8) ×3
  - deterministic-timeout trigger note (mutagen.mutators.r8 corollary) ×1

Verification stubs flipped: mutagen.mutators.v5 (new stub, execute: true,
pointing at case_drop_classification_test.exs)

Resolution choice: Option 1 (spec clarification). Reasoning:
- mutagen.decision.validate_predicates explicitly accepts validate/1 as
  "best-effort, not perfect" and the case_drop module's own @moduledoc
  documents the v1 conservative stance ("we conservatively skip nothing
  here so the runner's :survived count remains an honest upper bound").
- The runtime :killed classification IS correct: the cited test observably
  distinguished the dropped clause from the survivors via CaseClauseError.
  That's a real test signal, not a false positive.
- Option 2 (refining validate/1 to detect guarded-base-case patterns OR to
  strip guards on remaining clauses) would contradict the documented v1
  intent AND change the kill-rate denominator for an honest signal. It is
  the more expensive change and gains nothing testability-wise.

The clarification adds a new must-requirement (mutagen.mutators.r8) that
makes the classification rule falsifiable: the new test would fail if
either validate/1 started skipping these sites (returning {:skip, _}
instead of :ok) OR if Elixir's runtime behavior on a guarded-base-case
drop changed (no CaseClauseError raised on the dropped value).

Verification (local):
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors: clean
- mix test (full suite): 351 tests, 0 failures (was 347 pre-change; +4 new)
- mix test test/mutagen_ex/mutators/case_drop_classification_test.exs: 4/4 pass
- mix test test/mutagen_ex/mutators_test.exs id_stability_test.exs
  mutators_property_test.exs: 30/30 pass
- mix test test/mutagen_ex/mutators/ --only validate: 26/26 pass

Summary: Resolved DISCOVERY-D via spec clarification (option 1 per ticket
guidance). Added must-requirement mutagen.mutators.r8 + scenario s7 +
verification stub v5 + classification test pinning the documented contract.
Amended mutagen.mutation_pipeline.r5/s3 to direct deterministic-:timeout
fixtures at :arith on the recursive descent. The infinite_looper.ex
fixture's :arith trigger is now the spec's documented contract, not a
deviation that needs follow-up.

VERIFY: PASS
Session: agent-ab6f87529fde89873 (parent eb14f76b-662e-400e-ba6b-22e2f3772aea)
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex/eb14f76b-662e-400e-ba6b-22e2f3772aea/subagents/agent-ab6f87529fde89873.jsonl

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched: 5 (.spec/specs/mutators.spec.md, .spec/specs/mutation_pipeline.spec.md, lib/mutagen_ex/mutators/case_drop.ex, test/fixtures/lane_project/lib/lane_fixture/infinite_looper.ex, test/mutagen_ex/mutators/case_drop_classification_test.exs)
- Files outside Allowed globs: 0. Ticket lists Advances: mutagen.mutators and Resolution explicitly names mutagen.mutators / mutagen.mutation_pipeline. All five files align: two spec files for the advance + sister-subject clarification, one moduledoc-only edit to a surface listed under mutagen.mutators (case_drop.ex), one comment-only edit to the lane fixture that motivated the discovery, one NEW test file that pins the new r8 contract.
- Files matching Out-of-Scope globs: 0. No production behaviour was changed; case_drop.ex diff is moduledoc-only (verified with git diff filter); infinite_looper.ex diff is comment-only.

Semantic checks:
- Spec stubs run: 9, passed: 9
  - mutagen.mutators.v1 (mutators_test.exs): 15/15
  - mutagen.mutators.v2 (id_stability_test.exs): 4/4
  - mutagen.mutators.v3 (mutators_property_test.exs): 11/11
  - mutagen.mutators.v4 (--only validate): 26/26
  - mutagen.mutators.v5 (case_drop_classification_test.exs): 4/4  <-- new stub
  - mutagen.mutation_pipeline.v1 (baseline_test.exs): 8/8
  - mutagen.mutation_pipeline.v2 (mutation_runner_test.exs): 22/22
  - mutagen.mutation_pipeline.v3 (c1_test.exs): 1/1
  - mutagen.mutation_pipeline.v4 (c2_test.exs): 1/1
- Requirements coverage (focused on new/amended musts):
  - mutagen.mutators.r8: covered by case_drop_classification_test.exs:34/42 (validate -> :ok), :45/53 (CaseClauseError raised on dropped value), :56/62 (sanity: guard-matching path still evaluates), :67/79 (:arith corollary swap n-1 -> n+1). Would fail if CaseDrop.validate/1 started returning {:skip, :no_op_shadowed} for guarded-base-case sites, or if Elixir's runtime stopped raising CaseClauseError on clause-miss against a guarded survivor, or if Arith.mutate/1 stopped swapping (-) for (+).
  - mutagen.mutation_pipeline.r5 (amended wording): covered by mutation_runner_test.exs (classify/3 path) + new v5 stub. Would fail if MutationRunner.classify/2 (mutation_runner.ex:683-693) reclassified runtime exceptions in user code as :error instead of :killed.
  - mutagen.mutation_pipeline.s3 (amended scenario): covered by lane-project e2e + infinite_looper.ex fixture (test/fixtures/lane_project/lib/lane_fixture/infinite_looper.ex now uses :arith on count_down(n-1) as documented contract). Would fail if the :arith mutation in e2e ceased producing :timeout for the divergent-recursion case.
- Intent-level scope concerns: none. Option-1 (spec clarification) was chosen per ticket guidance; rationale in the IMPLEMENTED comment cites mutagen.decision.validate_predicates ('best-effort, not perfect') and is consistent with the existing case_drop.ex v1 conservative moduledoc.
- Project verification: pass (mix smoke = mix compile --warnings-as-errors clean; 351 tests, 0 failures, 13 excluded).
- Source-claim tracing for new/amended spec language: pass.
  - r8 claim ('validate/1 :ok + :killed via CaseClauseError') matches case_drop.ex:80-91 (validate_clauses only skips empty-clause lists) and mutation_runner.ex:683-693 (failures > 0 -> :killed). The new test calls real CaseDrop.mutate/1 + Code.eval_quoted/2 with n=0 and observes the actual CaseClauseError — not a mock.
  - r5 amended wording ('runtime exception in user code -> :killed, :error is reserved for crashes inside the test runner') matches mutation_runner.ex:683-703: only {:error, reason, meta} from MutationLoop (runner-level) routes to :error; ExUnit-caught exceptions land in :completed with failures>0 -> :killed.
  - s7 scenario traced: AST shape in the example matches CaseDrop.match?/1 (line 58-66, case with >=2 clauses); mutate drops the last (line 71-77); the surviving guarded clause raises CaseClauseError on the dropped value at runtime — confirmed by the v5 test.
  - s3 amended :arith trigger traced: infinite_looper.ex now starts count_down(n-1) with case head guard n > 0; Arith mutation flips n-1 to n+1 (verified by v5 test #4 and lib/mutagen_ex/mutators/arith.ex). On every iteration n stays > 0, no clause-miss fires, divergence is genuine, MutationLoop's Task.yield(timeout_ms) path then classifies :timeout.
- Audit-expanded scope: none.

Verdict: PASS

Notes:
- The v5 test asserts AST-level + Code.eval_quoted behaviour rather than driving the full mutation_runner end-to-end. That is appropriate for r8 (the requirement is about validate/1 + runtime-exception shape, which the test pins directly); the runner-level :killed outcome is already covered by mutation_runner_test.exs. If a future regression in classify/3 ever quietly remapped runtime exceptions to :error, the v5 test would still pass but mutation_runner_test would fail — that wiring is intact.
- All four touched non-test files agree with each other (spec language, moduledoc, fixture comment) — no internal self-contradictions in the deliverable.

AUDIT: APPROVE (auto — selective audit, orchestrated)

Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex/eb14f76b-662e-400e-ba6b-22e2f3772aea.jsonl

Reasoning: VERIFY reported PASS with full stub coverage (9/9 including the new
v5 stub for r8), full coverage of the new/amended must requirements (r8, r5,
s3) with concrete Would-fail-if lines, and end-to-end source-claim tracing
through case_drop.ex / mutation_runner.ex / infinite_looper.ex confirming the
new spec language describes code that actually exists. Production code diff is
moduledoc + fixture comment only (zero behavioural change); the only new
runtime artifact is a 4-test case_drop_classification test that calls real
CaseDrop.mutate/Code.eval_quoted (not mocks). Other r1-r7 requirements are
untouched by spec text and remain covered by their existing stubs. No auditor
was spawned per selective-audit policy.
APPROVED. Merged into epic/mutagen-wrd. Closing per orchestrator sign-off.

title: DISCOVERY: infinite_looper.ex uses :arith trigger, spec named :case_drop
